steps.title: Keep verb and object together
steps.content: The sentence needs the verb to connect directly to its object: Gillespie preferred the sound of a bent bell. Standard English does not place punctuation between a transitive verb and its direct object.

steps.title: Reject broken punctuation
steps.content: A uses a semicolon before the object, but the following words are not an independent clause. C inserts an incorrect comma between the verb and object. D creates a sentence fragment after the period. The remaining choice keeps the sentence grammatical. The answer is B.
